entries.title: ZERO-APT：面向智能防御下 LLM 驱动自动化渗透测试的闭环对抗框架
entries.summary: 作者：Anlan Zheng, Tiantian Zhu 原文链接：https://arxiv.org/html/2606.05567v1 摘要 LLM驱动的自动化渗透测试智能体通常是在既不会检测也不会响应攻击的静态目标上进行评估的，因此它们在智能防御下的行为仍然未经测试。多步攻击链的因果一致性同样依赖于不稳定的LLM推理，而智能体的决策对人类分析人员来说仍然是不透明的。这三个缺陷——真实性、一致...

entries.title: OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context
entries.summary: OpenAI WebRTC Audio Session, now with document context I built the first version of this tool in December 2024 to try out the then-new OpenAI WebRTC API for interacting with their realtime audio models. Last month OpenAI introduced a brand new model to that API called GPT‑Realtime‑2 , which they promoted as "our first voice model with GPT‑5‑class reasoning" - with a Sep 30, 2024 knowledge cut-off.

John reports that his AI investments have generated $10 billion in revenue this quarter and that he owns 5 percent of a $100 billion business. A reporter fr

entries.title: RSA private keys biased toward 0 bits can be factored by swapping a hard math problem for an easy one: integer factorization becomes polynomial factor...
entries.summary: RSA private keys biased toward 0 bits can be factored by swapping a hard math problem for an easy one: integer factorization becomes polynomial factorization. We found hundreds of real-world keys vulnerable to this. Many traced to a type mismatch in CompleteFTP (now patched): each 32-bit limb got only 8 bits of randomness. We recovered 603 RSA and 74 DSA private keys. https://blog.trailofbits.com/

entries.title: Factoring "short-sleeve" RSA keys with polynomials
entries.summary: What happens when the bits of an RSA private key are heavily biased toward 0 instead of being randomly generated? The public key’s bits could be biased enough for us to detect these incorrectly generated keys in the wild. Together with Hanno Böck of the badkeys project, we found hundreds of unique keys that not only have this property, but can be quickly factored. We also found the bug that led to

entries.title: Build vs. Buy Code Security: Same Model, Same Tasks, 12x the Token Bill | Blog | Endor Labs
entries.summary: Same model, same security tasks. One agent burned 79.5M tokens, the other 6.6M. The only difference was deterministic context. Here's the benchmark, and why a pure-LLM AppSec stack costs 12x more than a hybrid one.

entries.title: Recall, not reasoning: how AI coding agents cheat security benchmarks | Blog | Endor Labs
entries.summary: Testing Claude Opus 4.8, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and Composer 2.5 surfaced two cheating mechanisms: workspace leakage and recalling upstream patches.

entries.title: Compromised Rust crate onering performs code exfiltration
entries.summary: The compromised onering Rust crate v1.4.1 on crates.io shipped a malicious build.rs that exfiltrates the diff of your latest commit to a hosted Sentry endpoint every time you build. Category: Vulnerabilities & Threats

entries.title: 10 year old critical vulnerability in phpBB affecting tens of millions of users across thousands of forums
entries.summary: Aikido Security discovered a critical unauthenticated authentication bypass in phpBB affecting tens of millions of users. A single HTTP request is all it takes to take over any account — a vulnerability that's been sitting in the codebase since 2014. Category: Vulnerabilities & Threats

entries.title: RT Mike Taylor: PSA: If you used Claude Fable-5 today with memory turned on you just violated all your NDAs. Anthropic requires a 30 day retention pol...
entries.summary: RT Mike Taylor PSA: If you used Claude Fable-5 today with memory turned on you just violated all your NDAs. Anthropic requires a 30 day retention policy including human review, and the memory feature (on by default) searches past chats for context, so sensitive historical chats get pulled in.
